reasoning: Given the current observations, we can see that absorbance (A) is directly related to molar absorptivity (ε), concentration (c), and path length (l) through the Beer-Lambert law: A = εcl. Since we have the molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length, we can test by changing the concentration to see if the absorbance changes in a proportional manner. So, I will increase the concentration.

Since the current observation follows the Beer-Lambert law (A = εcl), I will decrease the path length to see how the absorbance changes. This will allow us to confirm the relationship between the variables.

The absorbance seems to be directly proportional to the concentration and the molar absorptivity, and inversely proportional to the path length, as per Beer-Lambert's law. Since we have confirmed this relationship, I will not make any further changes to the environment. The final equation is A = εcl.
